title: Lastrea erubescens
taxonomy: Lastrea erubescens is a species of fern in the family Athyriaceae. It is native to Asia and is also known as the red-stemmed lady fern. The classification of Lastrea erubescens is subject to ongoing research and some sources may group it differently. Some sources consider it to be a synonym of Athyrium filix-femina, while others recognize it as a distinct species. Further study is needed to confirm its classification with certainty.
uses: Lastrea erubescens, a type of fern, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help in treating wounds and skin infections. In some parts of the world, it is also used to treat respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis. The plant's extracts are believed to have antioxidant properties, which can help in protecting the body from oxidative stress and cell damage. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal plant.
